Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are going to model the insurance claim process as an accepting Petri net Steps 1 We have the following activities C Insurance Claim CR Claim Registration DV Document Verification FC Fraud Check CA Claim Assessment AP Approval P Payment N Notification CL Closure Additionally we have RDS Re Document Submission and II In depth Investigation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The process flow Start with C filing the claim to CR registration Then DV document verification

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: If documents are incomplete we go to RDS and then back to DV Actually the problem says before the process continues so we assume that after RDS we go back to DV to verify again

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Similarly for Fraud Check FC if it raises doubts we do II In depth Investigation and then we go back to FC The problem says this can happen multiple times

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So we have two loops Loop 1 DV to RDS to DV Loop 2 FC to II to FC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After the loops we proceed to CA then AP if needed then P then N then CL

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: In Petri nets we model this as a choice For simplicity we can have two paths after CA either go to AP if above threshold or skip to P if below

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We will model the process as follows Places We need places to represent the state after each step We will have a start place for the initial token and after each activity we have a place

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: At p after DV we have two possibilities If documents are complete we go to FC If incomplete we go to RDS and then back to DV

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: At p after FC we have two possibilities one to CA if no doubts and one to II followed by FC again if doubts

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: At p after CA we have two outgoing transitions one to AP if above threshold and one to P if below threshold Then after AP we must go to P Then after P we have N and CL

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The final marking should have a token in p after CL The initial marking should place a token in p start

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets define the places and transitions Places p start p after C p after CR p after DV p after RDS p after FC p after II p after CA p after AP p after P p after N p after CL Transitions t C t CR t DV t RDS t FC t II t CA t AP t P t N t CL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Arcs p start to t C to p after C p after C to t CR to p after CR p after CR to t DV to p after DV p after DV to t RDS to p after RDS p after RDS to t DV to p after DV p after DV to t FC to p after FC p after FC to t II to p after II p after II to t FC to p after FC p after FC to t CA to p after CA p after CA to t AP to p after AP p after CA to t P to p after P p after AP to t P to p after P p after P to t N to p after N p after N to t CL to p after CL

Name: Validation - IND
Text: However note we have reused the same transition t DV and t FC in loops That is acceptable A transition can have multiple input places

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets test the flow Start token in p start then C then CR then DV Then either FC or RDS and back to DV Then either CA or II and back to FC Then either AP then P or directly P Then N then CL final marking
